nodes.label: Integrated Windows security model
nodes.rationale: Every connection runs as the current Windows identity; SQL (dbatools) and REST (ReportingServicesTools / Invoke-RestMethod -WebSession) use the caller's own credentials so there are no SQL logins, passwords, or external secret store. The only secret is the encryption-key password, never stored in .env, passed as a [SecureString] -KeyPassword and prompted interactively (Read-Host -AsSecureString) when omitted.

nodes.label: SMB fileshare transfer model
nodes.rationale: Backups move over SMB fileshares, not any cloud object store. Phase 2 backs up .bak files to the source share, phase 3 copies them share-to-share, phase 4 restores from the target share keeping original DB names. Share roots and file names are always separate inputs so the cmdlets join <share>+<file> themselves and no full path is ever hard-coded.

nodes.label: Nine-phase migration runbook
nodes.rationale: Invoke-RsMigration sequences nine phases in-process (no child processes) in order, aborting on the first failure and throwing a terminating error naming the failing phase so a non-zero result identifies exactly where it stopped.

nodes.label: Idempotent subscription refresh
nodes.rationale: Each subscription is keyed on its content (owner, path, schedule, delivery) not its server-assigned Id, so re-running the import updates an existing match in place instead of creating a duplicate, making the step safe to repeat. Selective import via an -IncludeSubscription allow-list; empty means import all.
